---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# --- 2.3 Compare Alpha Diversity

# (This step should be done using the UNFILTERED processed data)

# Install and load the vegan package if you haven't already
# install.packages("vegan")
library(vegan)

# --- Prepare data for vegan ---
# The vegan package requires a data frame where ROWS are samples and COLUMNS are species.
# So, we need to transpose our data.

# Function to prepare a data frame for vegan
prepare_for_vegan <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    # Use species as the unique identifier. Remove rows with NA species.
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(species)) %>%
    # If multiple rows have the same species name, sum them up
    dplyr::group_by(species) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric), sum), .groups = 'drop') %>%
    # Set species as row names
    tibble::column_to_rownames("species") %>%
    # Transpose the data frame so samples are rows
    t()
}

illumina_for_vegan <- prepare_for_vegan(illumina_processed) # Using relative abundance
nanopore_for_vegan <- prepare_for_vegan(nanopore_processed) # Using counts before normalization *
# *Note: For simplicity here, we use the normalized data. For perfect accuracy with richness,
# you would start from the raw count data before the percentage conversion.

# --- 2.3.2 Calculate Shannon Diversity per sample ---
# --- Calculate Shannon Diversity ---
shannon_illumina <- diversity(illumina_for_vegan, index = "shannon")
shannon_nanopore <- diversity(nanopore_for_vegan, index = "shannon")

# --- Combine results into a tidy table ---
alpha_diversity_summary <- tibble(
  Sample = c(names(shannon_illumina), names(shannon_nanopore)),
  Sequencer = c(rep("Illumina", 4), rep("ONT", 4)),
  Shannon = c(shannon_illumina, shannon_nanopore)
)

print("--- Alpha Diversity (Shannon Index) ---")
print(alpha_diversity_summary)

# --- 2.3.3 Calculate Mean and SD of Shannon Diversity ---
# Group by sequencer and then calculate the mean and sd.
alpha_diversity_final_summary <- alpha_diversity_summary %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Sequencer) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Mean_Shannon = mean(Shannon),
    SD_Shannon = sd(Shannon),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

print("--- Mean and SD of Shannon Diversity by Sequencer ---")
print(alpha_diversity_final_summary)

# --- 2.3.4 Statistical Test for Shannon Diversity ---

# First, prepare the data by filtering for each sequencer
shannon_illumina_values <- alpha_diversity_summary %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sequencer == "Illumina") %>%
  dplyr::pull(Shannon)

shannon_nanopore_values <- alpha_diversity_summary %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sequencer == "ONT") %>%
  dplyr::pull(Shannon)

# Perform the paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test
# paired = TRUE is important because the samples are the same
wilcox_test_result <- wilcox.test(shannon_illumina_values, shannon_nanopore_values, paired = TRUE)

# Print the result
print(wilcox_test_result)


# --- 2.3.5 Visualize and Save Alpha Diversity Plot ---

# First, create the plot and assign it to an object named 'alpha_diversity_plot'
alpha_diversity_plot <- ggplot(alpha_diversity_summary, aes(x = Sequencer, y = Shannon, fill = Sequencer)) +
  
  # Add boxplot with black outline
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA))+
  
  # Add jittered individual data points for transparency
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2.5, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  
  # Use a grayscale palette for the fill colors
  scale_fill_grey(start = 0.6, end = 0.9) +
  
  # Set labels as requested
  labs(
    x = "Sequencer",
    y = "Shannon diversity index"
  ) +
  
  # Use a classic theme suitable for publications
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  
  # Fine-tune theme elements for better readability
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# --- Save the plot to a file using ggsave() ---

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/fig1_alpha_diversity_comparison.png", # File path and name
  plot = alpha_diversity_plot,                        # The plot object to save
  width = 5,                                          # Width of the plot in inches
  height = 4,                                         # Height of the plot in inches
  dpi = 300                                           # Resolution (dots per inch) for publication quality
)

# Finally, display the plot in your R Markdown output as before
print(alpha_diversity_plot)

```
